reasoning: The FRED series CCLACBW027SBOG has exhibited strong positive growth momentum over the last month (rising from ~1037 in early April to 1044.1 by May 21). Historically, intra-quarter momentum tends to be positive (growing about 1-2B per week) as credit card balances increase. Therefore, for the short-term resolution dates of June 1 and June 24, the probability of exceeding the May 25 baseline is fairly high due to this continuing upward trend. However, there were massive structural drops in the balance sheets at the very beginning of Q1 (January, -37B) and Q2 (April, -13B) 2025, which did not occur in the same way in 2024. If these represent a new trend of aggressive write-offs, loan securitizations, or other off-balance-sheet reclassifications at the start of quarters, Q3 (July) and Q4 (October) could see similar massive drops. The uncertainty of whether these sharp drops will repeat drastically lowers the probability for August and November, pulling them much closer to 0.5. Probabilities taper off accordingly to reflect increasing uncertainty at longer horizons.
